case_title: MIQ LOGISTICS, LLC VS. SUNLINK CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE CORRECT OR VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD (AMENDED PETITION) IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS ALREADY CONSIDERED AND DENIED RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO DISMISS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, CORRECT OR VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD. ON JUNE 20, 2014, AFTER HEARING ORAL ARGUMENT, THE COURT DENIED RESPONDENT'S ORIGINAL MOTION. ALTHOUGH PETITIONER HAS FILED AN AMENDED PETITION TO CORRECT A CLERICAL ERROR, THE SUBSTANCE OF THE PETITION AND THE ARBITRATION AWARD REMAIN THE SAME. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302/MJM)

APPLICATION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FILED BY PETITIONER MIQ LOGISTICS, LLC

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, CORRECT OR VACATE ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED. FIRST, THE MOTION IS UNTIMELY. A RESPONSE TO A PETITION TO CONFIRM AN ARBITRATION AWARD "SHALL BE SERVED AND FILED WITHIN 10 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF THE PETITION." (CCP SEC. 1290.6.) THIS INCLUDES A MOTION TO VACATE AN ARBITRATION AWARD FILED IN RESPONSE TO A PETITION. (OAKTREE CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, L.P. V. BERNARD (2010) 182 CAL.APP.4TH 60, 66.) THE ARBITRATION AWARD WAS DATED MARCH 16, 2014. THE PETITION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION WAS PERSONALLY SERVED ON RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION ("RESPONDENT") ON APRIL 1, 2014. RESPONDENT DID NOT FILE THIS MOTION UNTIL APRIL 30, 2014. EVEN IF THE COURT WERE TO REACH THE MERITS, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT ESTABLISH THAT THE AWARD WAS PROCURED BY FRAUD, AS REQUIRED BY CCP SECTION 1286.4. RESPONDENT ENTERED INTO A JOINT STIPULATION WHICH FORMED THE BASIS FOR THE ARBITRATION AWAY IN WHICH IT STIPULATED, AMONG OTHER MATTERS, THAT IT OWED THE PRINCIPAL AMOUNT OF THE INVOICES, PLUS INTEREST; AND REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS. (PETITIONER MIQ LOGISTICS, LLC OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DISMISS, EX. 1 ("JOINT STIPULATION").) FURTHER, THE ARBITRATOR DENIED RESPONDENT'S REQUESTS FOR CONSOLIDATION AFTER CONSIDERING THE ISSUE TWICE. "[T]HE MERITS OF A CONTROVERSY THAT HAS BEEN SUBMITTED TO ARBITRATION ARE NOT SUBJECT TO JUDICIAL REVIEW." (HARRIS V. SANDRO (2002) 96 CAL.APP.4TH 1310, 1313.) IN ANY EVENT, RESPONDENT DOES NOT ESTABLISH PREJUDICE BECAUSE THERE IS ANOTHER PENDING ARBITRATION AT WHICH THE REMAINING MATTERS CONCERNING THE PREPANELIZATION AGREEMENT WILL BE RESOLVED. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE AND THE REQUEST FOR STAY IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award (Amended Petition)
Off Calendar Aug-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES D. LAWRENCE OF BRYAN CAVE LLP, TEL. NO. (813) 374-3200 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCE ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award (Amended Petition) is Denied. The Court has already considered and denied Respondent's Motion to Dismiss, or in the Alternative, Correct or Vacate Arbitration Award. On June 20, 2014, after hearing oral argument, the Court denied Respondent's original motion. Although Petitioner has filed an amended petition to correct a clerical error, the substance of the Petition and the Arbitration Award remain the same. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES D. LAWRENCE OF BRYAN CAVE LLP, TEL. NO. (813) 374-3200 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. MARK GINALSKI OF SUNLINK, TEL. NO. 925-9650 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SUNLINK CORPORATION's Motion To Dismiss Or, In The Alternative, Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award is Denied. First, the motion is untimely. A response to a petition to confirm an arbitration award "shall be served and filed within 10 days after service of the petition." (CCP sec. 1290.6.) This includes a motion to vacate an arbitration award filed in response to a petition. (Oaktree Capital Management, L.P. v. Bernard (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 60, 66.) The arbitration award was dated March 16, 2014. The petition to confirm arbitration was personally served on Respondent SunLink Corporation ("Respondent") on April 1, 2014. Respondent did not file this motion until April 30, 2014. Even if the Court were to reach the merits, the motion is deniedhe evidence does not establish that the award was procured by fraud, as required by CCP section 1286.4. Respondent entered into a Joint Stipulation which formed the basis for the arbitration away in which it stipulated, among other matters, that it owed the principal amount of the invoices, plus interest; and reasonable attorney's fees and costs. (Petitioner MIQ Logistics, LLC Opposition to Motion to Dismiss, Ex. 1 ("Joint Stipulation").) Further, the arbitrator denied Respondent's requests for consolidation after considering the issue twice. "[T]he merits of a controversy that has been submitted to arbitration are not subject to judicial review." (Harris v. Sandro (2002) 96 Cal.App.4th 1310, 1313.) In any event, Respondent does not establish prejudice because there is another pending arbitration at which the remaining matters concerning the Prepanelization Agreement will be resolved. The court granted the Request for Judicial Notice and the Request for Stay is Denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or In The Alternative, Correct Or Vacate Arbitration Award

No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling.
